---
title: "TikZJax and Forest Test"
format:
  html:
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://tikzjax.com/v1/fonts.css">
          <script src="https://tikzjax.com/v1/tikzjax.js"></script>
  pdf:
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          \usepackage{tikz}
          \usepackage{forest}
execute:
  echo: false
---

# Simple TikZ Test

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
<script type="text/tikz">
out tikz 1
\begin{tikzpicture}
\node {out tikz in pic 1st};
\vspace{5}
\node[draw, rounded corners] {Hello TikZJax};
\node[draw, rounded corners] {Hello TikZJax 2};

\end{tikzpicture}
</script>
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}
\begin{tikzpicture}
\node[draw, rounded corners] {Hello TikZJax};
\end{tikzpicture}
:::

# Forest Syntax Tree

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
<script type="text/tikz">
    out tikz in
    \begin{tikzpicture}
    out tikz in picture
\node[draw, rounded corners] {Hello TikZJax};

\usepackage{forest}

\begin{forest}
[IP
  [NP
    [Det [meine]]
    [N$'$
      [N [Schwester]]
    ]
  ]
  [I
    [VP
      [V$'$
        [V [schreibt]]
        [PP
          [P [an]]
          [NP
            [Det [einem]]
            [N$'$
              [AP [wichtigen]]
              [N [Buch]]
            ]
          ]
        ]
      ]
    ]
  ]
]
\end{forest}
\end{tikzpicture}

</script>
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}
\begin{forest}
[IP
  [NP
    [Det [meine]]
    [N$'$
      [N [Schwester]]
    ]
  ]
  [I
    [VP
      [V$'$
        [V [schreibt]]
        [PP
          [P [an]]
          [NP
            [Det [einem]]
            [N$'$
              [AP [wichtigen]]
              [N [Buch]]
            ]
          ]
        ]
      ]
    ]
  ]
]
\end{forest}
:::

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis

library(tinytex)

forest_code <- "
[IP
  [NP
    [Det [meine]]
    [N$'$
      [N [Schwester]]
    ]
  ]
  [I
    [VP
      [V$'$
        [V [schreibt]]
        [PP
          [P [an]]
          [NP
            [Det [einem]]
            [N$'$
              [AP [wichtigen]]
              [N [Buch]]
            ]
          ]
        ]
      ]
    ]
  ]
]
"
f2 <- "
[IP[NP[Det [meine]][N$'$[N [Schwester]]]][I[VP[V$'$[V [schreibt]][PP[P [an]][NP[Det [einem]][N$'$[AP [wichtigen]][N [Buch]]]]]]]]]
"
f2<-gsub("[ \n]","",forest_code)
# Standalone LaTeX document
tex <- paste0(
'\\documentclass[tikz,border=2pt]{standalone}
\\usepackage{forest}
\\begin{document}
\\begin{forest}
', f2,'
\\end{forest}

\\end{document}
')

# File names
base <- "syntax-tree"
texfile <- paste0(base, ".tex")
pdffile <- paste0(base, ".pdf")
svgfile <- paste0(base, ".svg")

# Write and compile
writeLines(tex, texfile)
tinytex::latexmk(texfile)

# # Convert PDF to SVG for HTML
# if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
# #   system2("pdf2svg", c(pdffile, svgfile))
#   knitr::include_graphics(pdffile)
# } else {
#   knitr::include_graphics(pdffile)
# }
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
# after generating syntax-tree.pdf

if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
  # Read the PDF page size (in inches)
  info <- pdftools::pdf_info("./syntax-tree.pdf")
#   width_in  <- info$pages[[1]]$width  / 72   # points -> inches
#   height_in <- info$pages[[1]]$height / 72
  width_in <- pdftools::pdf_pagesize("syntax-tree.pdf")[2] / 72
  height_in <- pdftools::pdf_pagesize("syntax-tree.pdf")[3] / 72
  # Convert to CSS pixels (96 px = 1 inch)
  width_px  <- round(width_in  * 120)
  height_px <- round(height_in * 120)

  # Embed at the PDF's natural size
  cat(sprintf(
    '<object data="syntax-tree.pdf"
             type="application/pdf"
             style="width:%spx; height:%spx; border:none;"></object>',
    width_px, height_px
  ))
} else {
  # Use the PDF directly in PDF output
  knitr::include_graphics("syntax-tree.pdf")
}
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
# Convert a simple edge notation to LaTeX forest syntax
#
# Input format:
#   "IP->NP"
#   "NP->Det meine"
#   "NP->N$'$"
#   "N$'$->N schwester"
#
# Rules:
# - Left side is the parent.
# - Right side contains one or more children separated by spaces.
# - Repeated labels (e.g. multiple NP nodes) are allowed and treated
#   as distinct occurrences in the order they appear.
# - Child labels may contain LaTeX, e.g. N$'$, V$'$.
#
# Output:
#   A complete \begin{forest}...\end{forest} block.
lines<-"IP --> NP1 --> Det meene
NP1 --> N'1 --> N1 schwester
IP --> I --> VP --> V' --> V schreibt
V' --> PP --> P an
PP --> NP2 --> Det einem
NP2 --> N'2 --> AP wichtigen
N'2 --> N2 buch"
lines
to_forest <- function(lines) {
lines<-gsub("'","$'$",lines)
l2<-unlist(strsplit(lines,"\n"))
l2
x<-l2[1]
x
# Parse lines into parent -> children
  rules <- lapply(l2, function(x) {
    parts <- strsplit(x, "-->", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
    parts
    parent <- trimws(parts[1])
    rhs <- trimws(parts[2])
    children <- strsplit(rhs, "\\s+")[[1]]
    list(parent = parent, children = children)
  })

  # Root = first parent that never appears as a child
  all_parents  <- vapply(rules, `[[`, "", "parent")
  all_parents
  all_children <- unlist(lapply(rules, `[[`, "children"), use.names = FALSE)
  all_children
  root <- setdiff(all_parents, all_children)[1]
  root
  # Recursive expansion using occurrence order.
  # Each time a rule with a given parent label is used, it is consumed.
  used <- rep(FALSE, length(rules))
  used

  build <- function(label) {

    # Find next unused rule with this parent label
    idx <- which(!used & all_parents == label)

    # If no rule exists, this is a terminal node
    if (length(idx) == 0) {
      return(sprintf("[%s]", label))
    }

    # Consume the first matching rule
    i <- idx[1]
    used[i] <<- TRUE

    # Recursively build children
    child_txt <- vapply(
      rules[[i]]$children,
      build,
      character(1)
    )

    paste0(
      "[", label, "\n  ",
      paste(child_txt, collapse = "\n  "),
      "\n]"
    )
  }
b<-build(root)
#   paste(
#     "\\begin{forest}",
#     build(root),
#     "\\end{forest}",
#     sep = "\n"
#   )
b
}
# f2<-gsub("[ \n]","",lines)
f2<-to_forest(lines)
f2
mermaid_chain_to_forest <- function(txt) {
  txt<-gsub("'","$'$",txt)
  txt<-gsub("([A-Z'][0-9]{1,2})","$_\\1$",txt)
  txt <- gsub("\\r", "", txt)
  lines <- trimws(unlist(strsplit(txt, "\n")))
  lines <- lines[nzchar(lines)]

  parse_chain <- function(line) {
    parts <- trimws(unlist(strsplit(line, "-->", fixed = TRUE)))
    parts[nzchar(parts)]
  }

  chains <- lapply(lines, parse_chain)

  edges <- do.call(rbind, lapply(chains, function(x) {
    if (length(x) < 2) return(NULL)
    cbind(parent = x[-length(x)], child = x[-1])
  }))

  edges <- as.data.frame(edges, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  names(edges) <- c("parent", "child")

  roots <- setdiff(unique(edges$parent), unique(edges$child))
  children_map <- split(edges$child, edges$parent)

  build_tree <- function(node) {
    kids <- children_map[[node]]
    if (is.null(kids) || length(kids) == 0) {
      return(paste0("[", node, "]"))
    }
    paste0("[", node, " ",
           paste(vapply(kids, build_tree, character(1)), collapse = " "),
           "]")
  }

  paste(vapply(roots, build_tree, character(1)), collapse = " ")
}
f2<-mermaid_chain_to_forest(lines)
# Standalone LaTeX document
tex <- paste0(
'\\documentclass[tikz,border=2pt]{standalone}
\\usepackage{forest}
\\begin{document}
\\begin{forest}
', f2,'
\\end{forest}

\\end{document}
')
# File names
base <- "t3"
texfile <- paste0(base, ".tex")
pdffile <- paste0(base, ".pdf")
svgfile <- paste0(base, ".svg")

# Write and compile
writeLines(tex, texfile)
tinytex::latexmk(texfile)

# # Convert PDF to SVG for HTML
# if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
# #   system2("pdf2svg", c(pdffile, svgfile))
#   knitr::include_graphics(pdffile)
# } else {
#   knitr::include_graphics(pdffile)
# }


if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
  # Read the PDF page size (in inches)
#   info <- pdftools::pdf_info(texfile)
#   width_in  <- info$pages[[1]]$width  / 72   # points -> inches
#   height_in <- info$pages[[1]]$height / 72
  width_in <- pdftools::pdf_pagesize(pdffile)[2] / 72
  height_in <- pdftools::pdf_pagesize(pdffile)[3] / 72
  # Convert to CSS pixels (96 px = 1 inch)
  width_px  <- round(width_in  * 120)
  height_px <- round(height_in * 120)

  # Embed at the PDF's natural size
  cat(sprintf(
    '<object data="%s"
             type="application/pdf"
             style="width:%spx; height:%spx; border:none;"></object>',
    pdffile,width_px, height_px
  ))
} else {
  # Use the PDF directly in PDF output
  knitr::include_graphics(pdffile)
}
```


<math display="block">\Eta(X) := -\sum_{x \in \mathcal{X}} p(x) \log p(x),</math>
